original_prompt: Implement live JSON syntax highlighting for webhooks using Prism.js and a ghosting CSS architecture similar to the SQL editor. Update index.html, main.css, and view.triggers.js.
rephrased_goal: Integrate real-time JSON syntax highlighting into the Automation Rules Engine by layering transparent textareas over syntax-colored pre/code blocks, adding Prism JSON support, and establishing typographical synchronization via CSS.

tasks.description: Do-Pebble: Inject Prism JSON script into index.html
tasks.analogy_reference: giving cave magic glasses for JSON
tasks.testing_criteria: Script tag exists in index.html and URL is correct.

subtasks_required: 2.1 Inspect index.html near other prism components
tasks.testing_criteria: Prism JSON component script is present.

tasks.description: Do-Pebble: Add ghosting CSS to main.css
subtasks_required: 3.1 Append .json-editor-container and related styles to main.css
tasks.analogy_reference: digging shadow pit for two layers

subtasks_required: 4.1 Ensure font-family, line-height, and padding match between layers
tasks.testing_criteria: CSS properties are identical for both classes.

tasks.description: Do-Pebble: Update view.triggers.js with highlighting logic
subtasks_required: 5.1 Replace file with code containing syncJSONHighlight and HTML wrappers
tasks.analogy_reference: painting new masterwork with glowing JSON
tasks.testing_criteria: File exists with json-editor-container wrappers and syncJSONHighlight calls.

subtasks_required: Update wiki with live highlighting facts
tasks.testing_criteria: Build success, editor displays live colored JSON.
